column6: Beinn Stacach [Ceann na Baintighearna] [Stob Fear-tomhais] [Beinn Stacath]
column26: 1953-1984: Ceann na Baintighearna; 1990-1997: Stob Fear-tomhais; post-1997: Beinn Stacach, misspelt Beinn Stacath in the SMC guide to the Corbetts (2002)
Promoted to Corbett in May 2016 following height measurement by G&J Surveys and acceptance by the SMC

column6: Beinn Pharlagain [Ben Pharlagain - Meall na Meoig]
The Sow of Atholl [Meall an Dobharchain]

column26: GR change following appearance of 827m spot height on N top, 1m higher than S top
SMC online version of Munro's Tables has reverted to the S top; 2012 survey shows both summits are of equal height
Corbett moved from Creag an Dail Bheag to Carn Liath in 1990; both had 862m spot heights
Reinstated as Corbett  in 2015 following survey
Location moved 170m SE from NN428997 to NN429995 following publication of Corbett Tops (TACit 1999)
Grid reference in SMC guide to Corbetts (NH600145) is of the cairn which is not quite the summit position
New position given in 2002 edition of the SMC guide to the Corbetts; survey 2012 measured Buidhe Bheinn to be 0.29m higher than Sgurr a'Bhac Chaolais
Wrong summit identified, corrected in SMC guide to the Corbetts (2002)
Declassified as Twin Corbett with Hill 713 Buidhe Bheinn in November 2012
Summit moved in May 2001 following information from OS; current  maps show this summit as higher
Reclassified from Munro to Corbett in September 2009
Reclassified from Munro to Corbett in Nov 2012 following survey in 2010

column6: Beinn Liath Mhor a'Ghiubhais Li [Beinn Liath Mhor a'Ghiuthais]

column1: Ten-figure grid references of summits, information on summit features, details of surveys and more can be obtained from The Database of British and Irish Hills which may be downloaded from www.hills-database.co.uk
